title: forestiera pubescens var. neomexicana
intro: Forestiera pubescens var. neomexicana, also known as New Mexican boxwood or desert boxwood, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Forestiera pubescens var. neomexicana include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as colds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The herbal preparations of Forestiera pubescens var. neomexicana can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to create topical creams or salves for skin issues.
